titel: The Swedish National Audit Agency's report on the Swedish Gambling Authority's supervision of the gambling market.
html:
In this document, the government addresses the observations and recommendations made by the Swedish National Audit Agency (Riksrevisionen) in its report "The Gambling Authority's Supervision of the Gambling Market" (RiR 2024:19). The Swedish National Audit Agency's overall conclusion in the report is that the supervision of the gambling market is not sufficiently effective to meet the expectations expressed by the Parliament and the government during the deregulation of the gambling market in 2019. The Swedish National Audit Agency assesses that the Gambling Authority conducts too few inspections, does not select the companies subject to supervision based on well-founded risk analyses, and does not adequately follow up to ensure that identified shortcomings are rectified. The Swedish National Audit Agency also notes that the current scope of the Gambling Act (2018:1138) hinders efforts to combat illegal gambling.

The government welcomes the review and agrees with the Swedish National Audit Agency's assessment that the Gambling Authority has, at times, not had the resources to conduct supervision to a sufficient extent. The government has taken several measures to strengthen the authority's ability to conduct supervision and to ensure that reliable information about the scope and results of the supervision is provided. An expert has been commissioned to assist the Ministry of Finance in developing proposals for how the scope of the Gambling Act should be defined.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Agency's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Agency has reviewed the Swedish Gambling Authority (Spelinspektionen) and found that they are not adequately regulating the gambling market. The government agrees with this criticism and is now working to improve the controls and laws surrounding gambling.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors whether other government agencies are performing their duties correctly. In a new report, they state that the Swedish Gambling Authority, which is responsible for overseeing gambling companies, is not effective enough. They conduct too few inspections and do not adequately select the companies that pose the greatest risk.

There are also problems with controlling individuals who engage in illegal gambling. The current regulations in the gambling law make it difficult for the authority to stop illegal activities. The National Audit Office therefore concludes that the oversight does not meet the requirements set by the Parliament and the government.

The government agrees that the Swedish Gambling Authority has faced challenges in performing its duties effectively during certain periods. To address this, the government has already taken several steps to provide the agency with better opportunities to monitor the market. They also want to ensure that it is easier to obtain accurate information about how the oversight functions.

Currently, the Ministry of Finance, which is the part of the government responsible for economic matters, is working to amend the regulations. An expert has been commissioned to propose changes to the law that will make it easier to combat illegal gambling in the future.
